title: Validate USB descriptors against the spec
description: Check device, configuration, interface, and endpoint descriptors for consistent lengths and totals before enumeration testing.
body: Use when authoring or auditing USB device firmware. Do not rely on a single host accepting the device, since hosts vary in strictness. Actions: parse each descriptor, verify length fields and the configuration total length, and confirm endpoint addresses and packet sizes are consistent. Validation signals include a descriptor set that passes a compliance tool and enumeration succeeding across multiple host stacks. Risk boundary: an inconsistent total length can crash a strict host driver, so deny release until a compliance check passes.
